commit_message:
ci: ratify and document the single `verify` quality gate

Provide one `npm run verify` command so the ADW test gate can use
`ADW_TEST_CMD="npm run verify"`. The gate is shell-split and run via
spawnSync (no shell), so a chained `a && b` command cannot be used —
a single command is required.

- package.json: ratify `scripts.verify`, which chains
  typecheck -> lint:env -> pack:check -> test -> build -> rm -rf dist
  fail-fast via `&&`, ending by removing the dist/ build artifact.
- README.md (## Development): document `verify` as the canonical
  local/CI quality gate, show the `ADW_TEST_CMD="npm run verify"`
  single-command form, and link docs/LIVE-RUN-BATCH.md; keep the
  per-stage breakdown below it.
- test/scaffold.test.ts: add guard tests asserting the verify script
  chains the expected stages in canonical order, uses `&&`, ends with
  `rm -rf dist`, references only defined npm scripts, and that the
  README names `verify` as canonical with the ADW_TEST_CMD form and the
  batch-doc link.
- test/verify-gate.e2e.test.ts: assert the build/cleanup filesystem
  boundary (build creates dist/, `rm -rf dist` removes it; a failed
  build leaves no dist/).
- specs/verify-quality-gate-script.md: implementation spec.

Fix a typecheck failure in the new guard test: matchAll group access is
`string | undefined` under noUncheckedIndexedAccess, so the captured
script names are filtered to defined strings before assertion. Without
this, `npm run verify` failed at the typecheck stage.

Verified: `npm run verify` exits 0 (486 tests pass) and leaves no dist/.

closes #2
pr_body:
## Summary

Ratifies and documents a single `npm run verify` quality gate so ADW live
runs can set `ADW_TEST_CMD="npm run verify"`. The ADW test/finalize gates are
shell-split and run via `spawnSync(bin, args)` with **no shell**, so a chained
`a && b` command would pass `&&` as a literal argument and fail — one command
is required. `npm run verify` chains the gates internally via npm.

The `verify` script already existed from planning (`package.json:15`); this
change ratifies it, documents it, and guards it with tests.

## Changes

- **`package.json`** — ratified `scripts.verify` (no drift): chains
  `typecheck → lint:env → pack:check → test → build → rm -rf dist`, fail-fast
  via `&&`, ending with `rm -rf dist`.
- **`README.md` (`## Development`)** — documents `verify` as the **canonical**
  local/CI gate, shows the `ADW_TEST_CMD="npm run verify"` single-command form,
  and links [`docs/LIVE-RUN-BATCH.md`](./docs/LIVE-RUN-BATCH.md). The per-stage
  breakdown is kept below as the "what each stage does" reference.
- **`test/scaffold.test.ts`** — static guard tests: the `verify` script chains
  the expected stages in canonical order, uses `&&`, ends with `rm -rf dist`,
  and references only defined npm scripts; the README names `verify` as
  canonical with the `ADW_TEST_CMD` form and the batch-doc link.
- **`test/verify-gate.e2e.test.ts`** — runtime guard for the build-artifact
  lifecycle AC: `npm run build` creates `dist/`, `rm -rf dist` removes it, and a
  failed build leaves no `dist/`.
- **`specs/verify-quality-gate-script.md`** — implementation spec.

## Acceptance criteria

- [x] `npm run verify` runs all gates and exits non-zero if any fails (fail-fast
  `&&` chain).
- [x] `dist/` is removed at the end of a green run (no build artifact left).
- [x] README names `verify` as the canonical local/CI gate.

## Review note — blocker found and fixed

The new guard test broke the gate it protects: under `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`,
`matchAll` group access is `string | undefined`, so `toHaveProperty(name)`
failed `tsc --noEmit` and `npm run verify` exited red at the **typecheck**
stage. (It was invisible to a bare `vitest run`, which transpiles via esbuild
without typechecking.) Fixed by filtering the captured script names to defined
strings before the assertion.

## Verification

```
npm run verify   # exit 0 — 486 tests pass across 37 files; dist/ absent afterward
```

## Follow-ups (non-blocking)

- `test/verify-gate.e2e.test.ts` runs a real `npm run build` (~26s) inside the
  default `test` stage, so `verify` performs a full build twice (test stage +
  build stage) and plain `npm test` is ~26s slower. The `.e2e.test.ts` name
  implies separation, but there is no vitest project / `test:e2e` script, so it
  runs in the default suite. Consider gating it behind a separate `test:e2e`
  script (or vitest project) to keep the default gate lean.
- The "build exits non-zero for a missing tsconfig" e2e case exercises `tsc`'s
  generic behavior rather than the `verify` chain itself — low signal for its
  subprocess cost.
- Because the e2e build runs during the `test` stage, a broken
  `tsconfig.build.json` would first surface as a test-stage failure rather than
  a build-stage failure (minor failure-attribution confusion).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

review_findings.description: The new e2e test runs a full `npm run build` inside the default `test` stage, so `npm run verify` compiles the project twice (test stage + explicit build stage). This gate is run repeatedly in live ADW resolve loops (the issue's whole purpose), so it adds avoidable latency. The `.e2e.test.ts` name implies a separate suite, but there is no `test:e2e` script or vitest project, so it runs in the default `npm test`. Consider gating it behind a separate script/project to keep the recurring gate lean.

Second e2e case name/implementation mismatch: it('npm run build exits non-zero for a broken TypeScript source ...') does not run `npm run build`; it runs `npx tsc -p tsconfig.does-not-exist.json`, which is a missing-config error (not a broken source) and does not exercise the verify chain's `&&` fail-fast. Misleading label and low signal for its subprocess cost.

Both e2e cases are largely tautological (they validate tsc and the OS `rm` command) relative to the static guards in scaffold.test.ts, which already assert the chaining, canonical order, and `rm -rf dist` ending. Marginal incremental coverage of AC2's filesystem boundary.
